title: coffea malabarica
intro: Coffea malabarica, also known as the wild coffee or Malabar coffee, is a species of coffee plant native to Western Ghats in India and Southeast Asia. It is a small, evergreen tree that is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, headache, and rheumatism. The plant's leaves and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Coffea malabarica include infusions of the leaves and seeds, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments that are used to treat various health conditions.
